notes: Final-round audit after the round-9 regression and restore to the stronger round-8 implementation surface: Tiling remains accepted at the 64x384 hot-band shape; Coalescing Access, Data Reuse, Async Copy, and Pg2s remain baseline; L2 Cache, fixed-shape Stage, and the footprint-neutral B shared permutation are all now explicitly treated as tested-negative for this loop; the round-8 streaming B-consumer branch remains the best recent positive signal because it improved runtime and feed-side metrics without increasing registers or shared memory. That means the strongest remaining final-round bet is not another tiny B-layout permutation, but a more orthogonal complement on top of the restored round-8 feed branch. Recommended direction dir_01 therefore spends the last round on producer/consumer warp specialization over the round-8 streaming path: if the feed path is cleaner and the result is still occupancy-limited, the remaining upside is likely orchestration rather than another narrow layout tweak.

directions.name: Warp-specialized producer/consumer on top of the restored round-8 streaming B branch
directions.hypothesis: Round 8 showed that cleaning up the warp-local B consumer path can move runtime and feed metrics in the right direction without raising registers or shared memory. Round 9 then showed that squeezing the shared permutation harder is not the answer. The best remaining final-round bet is therefore to keep the restored round-8 streaming consumer branch and add the user's producer/consumer split on top: dedicate a small warp subset to staging and publication, while the remaining warps focus more heavily on MMA. This tests whether the residual gap is now CTA orchestration rather than raw B-fragment ordering.
directions.expected_bottleneck: All-warps staging and handoff overhead after the most obvious warp-local B feed pressure has already been reduced.
directions.risk: High but worthwhile for a final-round ceiling test. The branch is still register-limited to one resident block, so losing compute warps can erase the benefit if the producer count is wrong.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if barrier stall drops but hot-band time and runtime do not, or if tensor active falls because too many warps stop doing MMA.

directions.name: Human idea Ps2r on the restored round-8 streaming branch: one-fragment shared-to-register lookahead
directions.hypothesis: If the final round still wants to stay inside the feed family, the highest-ceiling remaining version is now Ps2r on the restored round-8 branch. That branch already streams one B fragment at a time in mirrored order, so the next direct feed-latency move is to overlap the next B fragment load from shared to registers with the current MMA issue chain, turning the existing consumer cleanup into a one-step register lookahead.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual shared-to-register feed latency inside the hot-band 64x64 micro-tile after the round-8 consumer refactor.
directions.risk: High register risk. The branch is already on the register wall, so a Ps2r lookahead can destroy the small positive signal round 8 recovered.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if registers rise or occupancy worsens without a clear hot-band runtime win.

directions.name: Trim the hot-band export path so the feed-side gains are not spent back in the epilogue
directions.hypothesis: If the restored round-8 feed branch is already close to the best point this loop found, the remaining tax may be on the way out rather than on the way in. The hot-band kernel still exports through the shared-memory scratch path, so a final alternative is to keep the round-8 B-feed branch and trim the `c_shared` export path or helper-warp writeback mechanics to reduce LSU/shared round-trip overhead without reopening tiling or B staging.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Epilogue/export LSU and shared-memory round-trip overhead after the feed path has already been partially improved.
directions.risk: High. The epilogue path is correctness-sensitive, and a scratch rewrite can easily balloon register pressure or produce subtle layout bugs this late in the loop.
directions.stop_condition: Reject if the export rewrite raises registers materially or leaves hot-band time flat.
